refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR includes multiple obligations: analysis of terrain, obstacles, and work-zone representation. To ensure atomicity and clarity, it is necessary to split these into separate sub-FRs, each focusing on a single aspect of worksite area analysis. This avoids conflating multiple requirements into one and maintains solution neutrality by focusing on what the system must do, not how.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Verify that the system can generate and provide a representation of the terrain within the worksite area, independent of obstacle or work-zone data.
sub_frs: The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including obstacle representation.
The NGHE solution shall provide worksite area analysis including work-zone representation.

candidate_dps: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite
Extract and represent key site features relevant to planning and execution
Integrate terrain and site features into a unified worksite map model
Provide an interface to share the worksite model with planning and execution functions
selected_dp.selected_dp: Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite

potential_sources: Integration of terrain and site features into a single model may create coupling if not modularized
Sharing the model with multiple functions requires clear interface definitions
coupling_check.notes: The selected DP focuses on creating a terrain model as a foundational element, which can be independently developed and then integrated with site features and shared via interfaces, supporting modularity and independence.

assumptions_risks: Assumes that terrain representation can be abstracted independently from other site features
Risk that site features extraction and integration may introduce coupling if not carefully modularized
Assumes planning and execution functions can consume the provided model format
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider explicitly separating the extraction of site features and the integration step into distinct DPs to further reduce coupling and clarify interfaces.

im_boundary.name: NGHE Solution
im_boundary.scope: The NGHE Solution module encompasses the processing and generation of digital terrain models representing the physical features of the worksite, including data acquisition, modeling, and output generation.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Receives sensor and environmental data necessary for terrain modeling.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Receives commands and parameters for terrain model generation.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Sends generated digital terrain models to other system components or external systems.

module_containment_scores.notes: High cohesion as terrain modeling is core to NGHE Solution; minimal diffusion as data inputs and outputs are well defined.
Low containment; vehicle focuses on mobility and control, not terrain modeling; would require significant diffusion to other modules.
Very low containment; teleoperation is for remote control, not data processing; high diffusion expected.
Minimal relevance; safety services unlikely to contain terrain modeling; high diffusion.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: The NGHE Solution module is the most appropriate and specific boundary to contain the digital terrain model generation DP, as it naturally includes data processing and environmental modeling functions, minimizing diffusion and maximizing cohesion.
containment_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality.
Integration challenges with other modules consuming the terrain model data.
Possible overlap with Vehicle sensors if terrain data acquisition is distributed.

fr_dp_gate.reason: The DP 'Generate a digital terrain model representing the physical features of the worksite' directly satisfies the FR requirement to provide worksite area analysis including terrain representation. The DP clearly addresses the functional need by specifying the creation of a digital terrain model, which is a concrete representation of the terrain features.

diffusion_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality may cause diffusion if data acquisition is distributed across modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: The NGHE Solution module has high containment (score 9) and low diffusion (score 2), indicating that the DP is well encapsulated within this IM boundary. The module encompasses data acquisition, modeling, and output generation, minimizing diffusion risk. Other modules have low containment and high diffusion scores, supporting the choice of NGHE Solution as the primary implementation module.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Verification is feasible as the NGHE Solution module outputs the digital terrain model via the Network Link interface, which can be validated against acceptance criteria requiring the model to be available to planning/execution functions.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: The allocation of the DP to the NGHE Solution IM is clear and well justified, with defined interfaces for input telemetry and tasking commands, and output of the terrain model, supporting traceability and ownership.

recommended_revisions: Monitor and mitigate risks related to telemetry data quality and availability to prevent diffusion of data acquisition responsibilities.
Clarify integration interfaces with other modules consuming the terrain model to ensure smooth data flow and reduce overlap.
Assess and document any overlap with Vehicle sensors if terrain data acquisition is distributed to avoid diffusion and maintain containment.

decision.justification: All gates (FR->DP, DP->IM, FR->IM) have passed with clear justification, high containment, low diffusion, and feasible verification. The DP directly satisfies the FR, and the IM allocation is well defined with clear interfaces and ownership. Although there are some diffusion risks related to telemetry data quality and availability, these are manageable through monitoring and mitigation rather than requiring further decomposition. Given the high safety criticality, maintaining a clear and contained implementation module is preferable to avoid complexity and diffusion. Therefore, no further sub-FR decomposition is needed at this stage.

revise.why: To monitor and mitigate risks related to telemetry data quality and availability, clarify integration interfaces, and assess overlap with vehicle sensors to prevent diffusion and maintain containment.

assumptions: Telemetry data acquisition is centralized within the NGHE Solution module.
Interfaces between modules consuming the terrain model are well defined and stable.
open_risks: Potential dependency on real-time telemetry data availability and quality causing diffusion if data acquisition is distributed.
Possible overlap with vehicle sensors in terrain data acquisition.
evidence_needed: Verification results of the digital terrain model output against acceptance criteria.
Documentation of integration interfaces and data flow between NGHE Solution and other modules.
Risk mitigation plans for telemetry data quality and availability.
